document.title: Risk Management Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk acceptability criteria, risk control verification, residual risk evaluation, and production/post-production information for the device.
keyPoints: The device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector classified as IIb with an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Applicable national and regional regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
The report applies EN ISO 14971:2019 risk management and identifies 43 risks before risk control.
Before risk control, 2 risks were acceptable and 41 risks were unacceptable.
After risk control, all 43 residual risks were evaluated in the acceptable region.
The first production and post-production information collection period was May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024.

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: residual risk risk remaining after risk control measures have been implemented

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: activities related to collection and review of relevant production and post-production information.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

evidence.claim: The report title is Risk Management Report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The covered model names are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
Revision 6 is dated Dec/31/2024 and updates production and post-production information.
evidence.evidenceText: 6 Dec/31/2024 Production and post-production information update

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on December 31, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by R&D team Han, Jimin December 31, 2024 Reviewed by R&D Manager Kim, Myeongseop December 31, 2024 Approved by Quality Manager Hur, Namkyu December 31, 2024

evidence.claim: The listed applicable national and regional regulations are EU MDR 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector classification

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector service life

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 43 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated. Acceptable region: 2 ea Unacceptable region: 41 ea

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 43 residual risks were acceptable and zero were unacceptable.
evidence.pageTarget: Residual risk evaluation after risk control

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria define risk levels 1 to 4 as acceptable and 5 to 25 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: Risk control implementation and effectiveness are verified through design/development procedures, usability testing, safety testing, and radio-related testing.
evidence.evidenceText: The verification of the implementation of each risk control measure follows: design and development verification according to ISO 13485... Usability test according to IEC 62366-1... Safety Test according to IEC 60601-1... Test related to RED (Directive 2014/53/EU)

evidence.claim: The overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: The first production and post-production information collection period ran from May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: 1st Information Collection Period: May 06, 2024(Initial CE marking) ~ December 31, 2024
evidence.pageTarget: Production and post-production information collection period

evidence.claim: 2024 sales quantities were reported for Korea, United States of America, India, UK, and Thailand.
evidence.evidenceText: 2024 40 Korea (domestic) 75 United States of America 16 India 12 UK 1 Thailand

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: source metadata signals FDA, but the document's applicable national and regional regulations list European Union and Republic of Korea; FDA appears only in incident feedback.
Some source text appears to contain OCR or transcription errors, including misspellings and inconsistent standard references such as EN 15322-1 where EN 15223-1 is otherwise listed.
